title: Glucose Tolerance Test in Mice
author: Jason Kim
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xxafpie

steps.instruction: Mice may be fasted overnight (~15 hours) or for 5 hours prior to the start of experiment.
Collect plasma sample (10 µl) before the start of experiment (basal-0 min) to measure basal glucose levels.
Administer intraperitoneal injection of 20% dextrose (1 or 2 g/kg body weight) using an insulin syringe.
Collect plasma samples (10 µl) at 10, 20, 30, 60, 90, and 120 min following injection to measure circulating glucose concentrations.
For data analysis, plasma glucose levels vs. time after injection are plotted, and areaunder-curve may be calculated to estimate insulin sensitivity.
Area-under-curve of glucose tolerance test may be inversely correlated with insulin sensitivity assuming unaffected insulin secretion and pancreatic β-cell function in mice.
